mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 6.0 percent signals labor market normalization, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives equity risk premiums lower, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year backtest, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction starts combined with expanding household credit balances indicate synchronized demand shocks that lift corporate revenue growth expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 60% of out-of-sample quarters, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An uptick in the industrial production index following a supply disruption confirms capacity restoration, which reduces input cost inflation and supports corporate margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO rises consecutively for two months, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms an overheating economy that forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate policy tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive forward returns in more than 55% of instances where GDP growth surpasses the threshold, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.1 over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in core inflation and the federal funds rate indicate a tightening monetary stance that outpaces economic growth, compressing equity risk premiums and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the cross-sectional correlation between the inflation-funds rate spread and forward equity returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained business hiring cycles that validate corporate revenue trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 10-year out-of-sample test, or forward returns show no statistical difference between positive and negative payroll growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.0 million annualized units while real GDP growth remains above 2.0 percent confirms a broad-based demand expansion that lifts cyclical equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a month.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces negative forward returns in more than 45% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for interest rate changes in a multivariate factor model.
